title: Spain in Every Season - When Is the Best Time to Visit?
description: Throughout the year, Spain stands as one of Europe's most diverse countries in terms of climate, landscapes, and culture. Each season offers a completely different experience depending on what you're seeking.
body:
Reading time: 8 minutes

Spain - A Destination for Every Season

Throughout the year, Spain stands as one of Europe's most diverse countries in terms of climate, landscapes, and culture. Each season offers a completely different experience depending on what you're seeking. Let's discover the best time to visit according to your preferred travel style. Before flying, don't forget your Sparks eSIM to stay connected wherever your journey takes you.

Spring (March to May)

The period from March to May brings pleasant temperatures ranging from fifteen to twenty-five degrees Celsius across most of the country. Flowers bloom everywhere creating a colorful countryside. Tourist crowds remain fewer than summer peaks, giving you more space to explore popular attractions. Prices for accommodation and flights are reasonable compared to summer. Andalusia shines brightest, with Seville hosting the famous Feria de Abril festival filling the city with flamenco, horses, and traditional dress. Almond tree blossoms blanket the Sierra Nevada mountains in white and pink.

Summer (June to August)

Daylight hours extend long into the evening. There is a vibrant atmosphere in the streets with locals enjoying outdoor dining and entertainment. The Balearic Islands become popular; Mallorca is beautiful, and Ibiza's legendary clubbing scene draws people from around the world. Menorca is a quiet, family-friendly alternative. The Mediterranean coast like Costa Brava prospers, and Valencia is an excellent urban destination. Marbella exudes luxury with upscale resorts and restaurants. San Sebastian has amazing food in the Basque region with surf opportunities. Central Spain gets very hot, often over thirty degrees Celsius, so many Spaniards go on vacation and hotels will be full.

Autumn (September to November)

Autumn is special. La Rioja is magical for the grape harvest and fresh vintages. Medieval Spanish history in Segovia and Avila is at its best. The changing colors and golden walls make for great photography. Cities like Cordoba and Granada, along with the Alhambra, are cooler. Darkness limits evening sightseeing, and you should begin packing rain-proof clothing.

Winter (December to February)

Museums continue to be peaceful, like Gaudi's works or Bilbao's Guggenheim. The sea slopes look relatively mild. The Canary Islands off Africa offer zero disappointment with mild weather, fitting for a romantic trip.
